library.name: Slow Build → Deep Encode
library.short_description: Gradual attention, language and memory engagement, late reasoning climax
library.description_template: Gradual attention building over the runtime, deep language network engagement, memory encoding throughout, climax in a Reasoning Beat in the back half.

fingerprint.chord_timing_pattern: chords cluster in second half of runtime

exemplar_videos: Cleo Abram - Huge If True - Quantum Computing intro
Vox - The Search for the Meaning of the Universe
Veritasium - The Discovery That Transformed Pi

library.name: Visceral Hook → Low-Effort Hold
library.short_description: Immediate insula spike, sustained low cognitive load, late emotional close
library.description_template: Immediate anterior insula spike in the opening seconds, sustained low cognitive effort across the middle, late Emotional Impact in the closing third.

fingerprint.chord_timing_pattern: visceral hit in first 10s, emotional impact in last 15s

exemplar_videos: MrBeast - Last to Leave Circle Wins $500,000

library.name: Personal → Social → Story
library.short_description: Self-relevance opens, theory-of-mind takes over, narrative integration sustains
library.description_template: Self-relevance opens the runtime, theory-of-mind activates as characters appear, narrative integration sustains the back half through the close.

fingerprint.chord_timing_pattern: personal resonance peaks first 15s, social peaks 20-40s

library.name: Cold Cognitive Work
library.short_description: High effort and language without personal or visceral engagement
library.description_template: High dorsolateral prefrontal effort and language network engagement throughout, with self-relevance and visceral response staying low across the full runtime.

fingerprint.chord_timing_pattern: reasoning beats spread across runtime, no visceral or emotional chords

library.name: Feel → Think → Feel
library.short_description: Visceral open, reasoning middle, emotional close — the classic three-act
library.description_template: Visceral hit in the opening, reasoning beat in the middle, emotional impact at the close. The three-act persuasion structure mapped onto the cortex.

fingerprint.chord_timing_pattern: visceral first 15s, reasoning 20-40s, emotional last 15s — in order

library.name: Setup → Reveal
library.short_description: Sustained attention and theory-of-mind building, late visceral spike
library.description_template: Sustained attention and theory-of-mind building over most of the runtime, late visceral spike in the closing seconds. The narrative payoff structure.

fingerprint.chord_timing_pattern: no chords in first 40s, visceral or emotional chord in last 20s
fingerprint.rate_of_change: gut reaction shows sharp inflection in last 20%

library.name: Pure Attention Spike
library.short_description: Strong attention without recruitment of any other cognitive system
library.description_template: Strong dorsal attention network activation without recruitment of language, memory, or self-relevance systems. Attention captured but not converted into deeper engagement.

library.name: Empty Calories
library.short_description: Visceral firing without any other cortical engagement — body without mind
library.description_template: Sustained visceral response without recruitment of memory, language, or self-relevance systems. The body reacts; the mind disengages. Often diagnostic of bad incentives.

library.name: Talking Head Reasoning
library.short_description: Language and effort with moderate social — straight argument delivery
library.description_template: Language network and dorsolateral prefrontal effort engaged throughout, moderate theory-of-mind activation tracking the speaker. Argument delivery, no visceral or memory peaks.

library.name: Cinematic Build
library.short_description: Sustained attention with periodic social and emotional peaks
library.description_template: Sustained attention across the full runtime with periodic peaks in theory-of-mind and emotional impact. Story-driven, character-anchored, paced for sustained immersion.

library.name: Rapid Cuts, No Anchor
library.short_description: Attention spikes without any sustaining system coupling
library.description_template: Repeated attention spikes from rapid scene changes, but no coupling to memory, language, or emotional systems. Each spike resets the previous one.

library.name: Emotional Arc, No Reasoning
library.short_description: Personal resonance and visceral response high, cognitive control low
library.description_template: Personal resonance and visceral response activated together throughout, with cognitive control staying low. The viewer feels without analyzing.

library.name: Social Modeling Heavy
library.short_description: Theory-of-mind dominant, other systems supporting role
library.description_template: Right temporoparietal junction activation dominant across the runtime — the cortex is constantly modeling other minds. Other systems play supporting roles.

library.name: Language-Heavy Narration
library.short_description: Sustained language network with attention but limited effort
library.description_template: Sustained language network activation with attention engaged, but cognitive control staying low. Voice-driven content the viewer follows passively.

library.name: Memory-Anchored
library.short_description: Memory encoding peaks repeatedly, anchoring the experience
library.description_template: Memory encoding peaks at multiple points across the runtime, anchoring specific moments for later recall. Built around discrete memorable beats.

library.name: Tight Integration, Balanced Pull
library.short_description: All seven systems coordinating tightly, no single system dominant
library.description_template: High integration score with all seven systems coupling at moderate to strong levels. No single system dominates — the cortex operates as a unified network throughout.

library.name: Decoupled Parallel Pipelines
library.short_description: Multiple systems firing strongly but independent of each other
library.description_template: Several cortical systems show strong activation, but with low pairwise coupling. Each system operates in its own lane — the cortex processes the content as parallel streams rather than an integrated experience.
exemplar_videos: music video with rapid context switches

library.name: Uncategorized
library.short_description: Cortical signature does not match any current library entry
library.description_template: This video's cortical signature does not match any recipe currently in the Brain Diff library at confidence ≥ 0.6. Closest match noted in audit logs.
